records.ety: [OE. pitance, pitaunce, F. pitance; cf. It. pietanza, LL. pitancia, pittantia, pictantia; perh. fr. L. pietas pity, piety, or perhaps akin to E. petty. Cf. Petty, and Pity.]

defs: An allowance of food bestowed in charity; a mess of victuals; hence, a small charity gift; a dole.
quotes: One half only of this pittance was ever given him in money.

defs: A meager portion, quantity, or allowance; an inconsiderable salary or compensation.
quotes: The inconsiderable pittance of faithful professors.
